vulnerability.reference: Human decision-makers override algorithmic corrections, reintroducing historical biases when trust in the system is low.
vulnerability.generated: Groups with systematically biased observed utilities have no means to challenge misestimated scores when bias correction uses unverifiable distributional assumptions.

impact.reference: Reinforcement of historical disparities in educational access, limiting social mobility for underrepresented groups.
impact.generated: Biased groups systematically receive fewer preferred placements, lowering true utility and entrenching group disparities in access to desired institutions.

dose_response.reference: Mis-specified bias models in centralized selection misallocate admissions when human overrides reintroduce historical biases, exposing applicants to reinforced disparities that limit social mobility for underrepresented groups.
dose_response.generated: Centralized fair selection correcting biased utilities via distributional assumptions, through AI-based scoring and ranking, triggers inability to challenge misestimated scores for groups with biased utilities, leading to fewer preferred placements and entrenched group disparities.
